description: Cloud object storage, file sync/sharing, and backup services for application and personal data.

entries.name: Amazon S3
entries.summary: Industry-standard object storage with virtually unlimited scalability and deep AWS ecosystem integration.
entries.freeTier: 5GB standard storage, 20,000 GET / 2,000 PUT requests free for 12 months.

entries.name: Cloudflare R2
entries.summary: S3-compatible object storage with zero egress fees, ideal for serving large files without bandwidth surprises.
entries.freeTier: 10GB storage free/month, 1M Class A and 10M Class B operations free/month.
entries.paidFrom: $0.015/GB storage beyond free tier; no egress charges ever.

entries.name: Backblaze B2
entries.summary: Low-cost S3-compatible object storage, popular for backups and media storage with free egress to many CDNs.

entries.name: Wasabi
entries.summary: Flat-rate S3-compatible cloud storage with no egress or API request fees.
entries.freeTier: 30-day free trial with 1TB; no perpetual free tier.

entries.name: Google Cloud Storage
entries.summary: Google's object storage with multiple storage classes (Standard, Nearline, Coldline, Archive) for tiered cost.
entries.freeTier: 5GB-months Standard storage free, 5,000 Class A and 50,000 Class B ops free/month.

entries.name: Uploadcare
entries.summary: File uploading, storage, and on-the-fly image/video processing API with a CDN-backed delivery layer.
entries.freeTier: Free tier includes limited monthly uploads and storage.

entries.name: Cloudinary
entries.summary: Media management platform for image/video upload, storage, transformation, and optimized delivery.
entries.freeTier: 25 monthly credits free, covering a generous mix of storage, transformations, and bandwidth.

entries.name: ImageKit
entries.summary: Real-time image and video optimization/CDN service with URL-based transformations.

entries.name: Dropbox
entries.summary: File sync and sharing service, also offers a developer API for app-integrated storage.

entries.name: Filebase
entries.summary: S3-compatible storage backed by decentralized networks (IPFS, Sia, Storj) accessed through a standard API.
